text: (After the final exams, you get a report card.)
(Some people are affected by it, filled with anxiety, frustration, or relief.)
Press the right arrow key to turn your head
Press left to see the window.
empty (you should not seen this text)
Let's continue. Everyone knows the word "July," right?
(Add an **'an'** at the end. In Latin, this means 'belonging to July'.)
(When I was studying abroad, on a beach over there, I told myself: Julian, you must be like the scorching sun of July, burning away all mediocrity and cowardice!)
(English is the easiest language in the world! As long as you dare to shout it out, you can overcome it, and overcome yourself.)
(The students begin to speak timidly, but it's clear the teacher is still not satisfied.)
I'm sure everyone will be able to go beyond English by the end of summer.
Please bring your Go Beyond English textbook; we'll start the first lesson next time.
Feel free to explore in any of the four directions.
Don't forget to press the down arrow key to collect your textbooks.
